title: Make a URL ownership declaration
description: Declare that you legally own a website domain (URL) registered to your company.
summary: Sometimes a business needs to prove that it legally owns a particular website domain – for example, when it is being sold, when it is acquired, or when it is used as a trademark.
The URL declaration is a sworn statement made before an attorney-at-law. The declarant confirms that the URL is registered to and beneficially owned by the company.
Bring a WHOIS print-out or registrar receipt as proof of the URL’s registration. The attorney-at-law witnesses your signature and stamps the document.
There is no government fee. Your attorney-at-law may charge their normal fee for preparing and witnessing the declaration.

items: the URL you are declaring (for example, www.yourbusiness.bb)
the legal name of the company that owns the URL
the date the URL was registered

items.name: Proof of URL registration
items.note: A WHOIS print-out or registrar receipt.

fees: No government fee. Your attorney-at-law may charge for witnessing the declaration.

options.heading: With an attorney-at-law
content: Make the declaration before a Barbadian attorney-at-law, who will witness your signature and stamp the document.

contact.agency: Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO)
contact.address:
Baobab Tower, Ground Floor
Warrens
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘No government fee’ is inferred – URL declarations are not usually filed with a public authority.
‘Same day with your attorney-at-law’ is an estimate based on common practice.
